title: THE HISTORY OF THE AMERICAN BASEBALL HAT
chaptersLine: Forty-plus chapters drafted, plus an introduction and a closing coda.
spanLine: From before the Civil War to the drops of 2026.
thisWeek: Folded in a long sit-down with one of the makers who actually manufactures the custom caps.
Ran down a century-old language curiosity and confirmed it, without giving away where it lands.
Filed three brand-new 2026 releases as live evidence the story is still being written in real time.
chapterHighlights: The 1996 World Series phone call that turned one red Yankees cap into the whole custom-color economy.
The 2007 season the wool went away and the underbill quietly turned black for good.
The Minnesota farmer who gathered more than one hundred thousand free caps, one giveaway at a time.
The mall chain that bet a store could survive on hats alone, and was right.
